Pete, a security analyst, has been tasked with explaining the different types of malware to his colleagues. The two malware types that the group seems to be most interested in are botnets and viruses. Which of the following explains the difference between these two types of malware?
opts: Viruses are a subset of botnets which are used as part of SYN attacks.
Botnets are a subset of malware which are used as part of DDoS attacks.
Viruses are a class of malware which create hidden openings within an OS.
Botnets are used within DR to ensure network uptime and viruses are not.
a: Botnets are a subset of malware which are used as part of DDoS attacks.

q: It is usually done by flooding the targeted machine or resources with superfluous requests to overload the system.
An administrator notices an unusual spike in network traffic from many sources. The administrator suspects that:
opts: it is being caused by the presence of a rogue access point.
it is the beginning of a DDoS attack.
the internal DNS tables have been poisoned.
a: it is the beginning of a DDoS attack.

q: It is a method used to gain access to data, systems, or networks, primarily through misrepresentation.
A network administrator noticed various chain messages have been received by the company. Which of the following security controls would need to be implemented to mitigate this issue?
Which of the following could a security administrator implement to mitigate the risk of tailgating for a large organization?
opts: Train employees on correct data disposal techniques and enforce policies.
Only allow employees to enter or leave through one door at specified times of the day.
Only allow employees to go on break one at a time and post security guards 24/7 at each entrance.
Train employees on risks associated with social engineering attacks and enforce policies.
a: Train employees on risks associated with social engineering attacks and enforce policies.

q: It is an unskilled individual who uses scripts or programs, such as a web shell, developed by others to attack computer systems and networks and deface websites.

It addresses any information that is protected against unwarranted disclosure.
Mandatory vacations are a security control which can be used to uncover which of the following?
opts: Fraud committed by a system administrator
The need for additional security staff
a: Fraud committed by a system administrator

q: The fundamental information security principals include confidentiality, availability and which of the following?
opts: The ability to secure data against unauthorized disclosure to external sources
The capacity of a system to resist unauthorized changes to stored information
The confidence with which a system can attest to the identity of a user
The characteristic of a system to provide uninterrupted service to authorized users
a: The capacity of a system to resist unauthorized changes to stored information
